question_text: A uniform beam is supported at a pivot. A 300 N load hangs 0.8 m to the left of the pivot. Two forces act to the right: force A (150 N) at 1 m from the pivot, and force B at 2 m from the pivot. For the beam to be in equilibrium, what must force B be?
explanation: Clockwise moment from the 300 N load: 300 × 0.8 = 240 Nm. Anticlockwise moments must equal this: (150 × 1) + (B × 2) = 240. So 150 + 2B = 240, which gives 2B = 90, and B = 50 N. However, rechecking: both forces to the right act anticlockwise, so 150 × 1 = 150 Nm and B × 2 must equal 240 - 150 = 90 Nm, so B = 45 N. Actually: 150 + 2B = 240, so B = 45 N.
